# note priority: add/edit/clear + list filter/sort
mkdir proj
cd proj
exec mtt init
stdout 'initialized'

exec mtt note add zeta --priority high
stdout 'created zeta'
exec mtt note show zeta
stdout 'priority: high'

exec mtt note add alpha
stdout 'created alpha'

# list --priority filters on the stored label (only zeta is high)
exec mtt note list --priority high
stdout 'zeta  \(untitled\)'
! stdout 'alpha  \(untitled\)'

# default order is recency; same-second creations tiebreak by slug asc -> alpha, zeta
exec mtt note list
stdout '(?s)alpha  \(untitled\).*zeta  \(untitled\)'
# --sort priority puts zeta (high, rank 0) before alpha (unset, rank medium)
exec mtt note list --sort priority
stdout '(?s)zeta  \(untitled\).*alpha  \(untitled\)'

# clear the priority
exec mtt note edit zeta --priority ''
stdout 'updated zeta'
exec mtt note show zeta
! stdout 'priority:'

# invalid --priority is a usage error (exit 1)
! exec mtt note add c --priority bogus
stderr 'invalid --priority'
! exec mtt note edit alpha --priority bogus
stderr 'invalid --priority'

# invalid --sort
! exec mtt note list --sort nonsense
stderr 'invalid --sort'

# priority surfaces in --json (omitempty)
exec mtt note add p --priority medium --json
stdout '"priority": "medium"'
